name: Amethyst
description: A stone of spiritual protection and purification — calms the mind, heightens intuition, and creates a shield against psychic manipulation.

name: Aquamarine
description: A stone of the sea carried by sailors for protection. It clears the mind, strengthens communication, and connects the practitioner to water spirits and the deep unconscious.

name: Black Obsidian
description: Volcanic glass that cuts through illusion mercilessly. Used in scrying mirrors, shadow work, and for severing energetic cords. It does not soften its revelations.

name: Black Tourmaline
description: One of the strongest protective stones — placed at the four corners of a home or carried to create an impenetrable energetic shield against psychic attack.

name: Carnelian
description: A stone of fire that dispels apathy and ignites the will to create. Carried by warriors and artists alike for courage, confidence, and the drive to act.

name: Citrine
description: The merchant's stone — placed in the cash register or wallet to attract abundance. Unlike most crystals it does not absorb negative energy but dissipates it.
safety: Natural citrine is rare; most commercial citrine is heat-treated amethyst. Both work effectively.

name: Clear Quartz
description: The master crystal — amplifies the energy of any stone or intention placed near it. Can be programmed for any purpose and substituted for any other crystal.

name: Fluorite
description: Brings structure to chaotic thought and cuts through confusion and manipulation. Excellent for students and for protecting the mind during psychic work.

name: Garnet
description: A stone of commitment and primal vitality — worn to attract loyal love, to sustain the practitioner through long spiritual work, and to regenerate depleted energy.

name: Hematite
description: Dense and grounding, hematite anchors scattered energy back to the body. Used to strengthen physical and energetic boundaries and to boost willpower.

name: Jade
description: Sacred throughout East Asian traditions for its connection to virtue and good fortune. Worn to attract luck, protect the wearer, and bring harmonious relationships.

name: Labradorite
description: The stone of magic — it reveals what is hidden, protects the aura from energy leaks, and opens the practitioner to the full spectrum of synchronicity and mystery.

name: Lapis Lazuli
description: A royal stone used by priests and pharaohs to commune with the divine. It stimulates higher wisdom, unveils truth, and opens the inner eye for visionary work.

name: Lepidolite
description: A stone of transition and peace — accompanies the practitioner through major life changes and dissolves anxiety without sedating awareness.

name: Malachite
description: An intense amplifier of emotions and intentions — it draws out what is ready to be released and transforms stuck energy. Protective especially during travel.
safety: Toxic when powdered or wet. Never make elixirs with raw malachite. Tumbled stones are safe to handle.

name: Moonstone
description: The stone of the Moon, attuned to cycles, tides, and feminine mysteries. Worn for psychic protection during travel and to honour the turning of the wheel.

name: Pyrite
description: Fool's Gold — but no fool's stone. It shields against environmental and emotional pollution, ignites willpower, and strongly supports manifestation of physical goals.

name: Rhodonite
description: Bridges the heart and the will — for those doing the difficult work of forgiving without forgetting, and translating compassion into concrete, grounded action.

name: Rose Quartz
description: The stone of universal love — placed on the heart chakra in healing work, worn to attract love, and given as a gift of genuine care and compassion.

name: Selenite
description: A high-vibration crystal of pure light — used to cleanse other crystals (without needing cleansing itself), open the crown chakra, and establish clear channel to higher guidance.
safety: Water soluble — do not submerge or cleanse with water.

name: Smoky Quartz
description: Transmutes negative energy into neutral Earth energy rather than simply deflecting it. Excellent for anyone doing shadow work or navigating grief and depression.

name: Sodalite
description: Balances rational thought with intuition and is particularly useful for those who distrust their own inner voice. Encourages calm, honest communication.

name: Tiger's Eye
description: A stone of grounded courage — it helps the practitioner see through illusion and deception, act from a place of integrity, and remain centered under pressure.

name: Turquoise
description: One of the oldest protective talismans — worn by warriors, shamans, and rulers alike. Guards against harm, opens honest communication, and attunes the spirit.
safety: Avoid contact with oils, perfumes, and water as it is porous and can be damaged.
